title: Security Incident Triage
description: Analyze security logs and triage a potential breach incident.
scenario: You are a SOC analyst. At 2:47 AM, automated alerts fired for unusual activity. Review the logs below and perform incident triage.
logs: 2024-01-15 02:31:14 - AUTH - user: admin@company.com - LOGIN_FAILED - IP: 185.220.101.45 - attempt 1
2024-01-15 02:31:22 - AUTH - user: admin@company.com - LOGIN_FAILED - IP: 185.220.101.45 - attempt 2
2024-01-15 02:31:31 - AUTH - user: admin@company.com - LOGIN_FAILED - IP: 185.220.101.45 - attempt 3
2024-01-15 02:32:01 - API - GET /api/users?limit=10000 - user: admin@company.com - IP: 185.220.101.45 - 200 OK - 2.3MB
2024-01-15 02:32:15 - API - GET /api/users/export?format=csv - user: admin@company.com - IP: 185.220.101.45 - 200 OK - 8.7MB
2024-01-15 02:32:44 - API - GET /api/payments/history?all=true - user: admin@company.com - IP: 185.220.101.45 - 200 OK - 4.1MB
2024-01-15 02:33:12 - API - POST /api/users/bulk-update - user: admin@company.com - IP: 185.220.101.45 - 200 OK
2024-01-15 02:34:01 - AUTH - user: admin@company.com - LOGIN_SUCCESS - IP: 192.168.1.105 - (normal office IP)

additionalContext.ip185Info: Known Tor exit node, flagged in threat intelligence feeds
additionalContext.adminNormalHours: 9am-6pm EST, never logged in at 2am before
additionalContext.dataExported: ~15MB total — full user database + payment history

questions: What type of attack occurred? Classify the incident severity (P1/P2/P3/P4).
What is the timeline of the attack? What did the attacker accomplish?
What immediate containment actions should you take RIGHT NOW?
What evidence should you preserve and how?
What notifications are required (legal, regulatory, users)?
evaluationCriteria: Correctly identifies credential stuffing + data exfiltration
Immediate actions: disable account, block IP, revoke sessions
Mentions log preservation and forensic chain of custody

title: Vulnerable Code Review
description: Review the following code for security vulnerabilities and provide remediation.
scenario: You are reviewing a pull request for a Node.js REST API. Identify all security vulnerabilities and explain how to fix them.
vulnerabilities: SQL Injection in /user endpoint (unsanitized query parameter)
No error handling (leaks DB errors to client)
No rate limiting on login endpoint
SELECT * exposes all user fields including sensitive data
questions: List all security vulnerabilities you find, ordered by severity.
What security headers should this API include?
How would you implement proper authentication for this API?
